data.headline: Home Remodeling in Branford, Connecticut
data.subheadline: Serving Branford homeowners from our Orange, CT headquarters — full-service remodeling for kitchens, bathrooms, basements, and flooring.

data.title: Home Remodeling in Branford, CT
data.content:
BuiltWell CT serves Branford, Connecticut from our headquarters at 206A Boston Post Road in Orange — about 20 minutes from our Orange office. We handle every project in-house with our own crews, never subcontracting your work to unknown teams.

Whether you're planning a kitchen renovation, a bathroom update, a finished basement, or new flooring, we bring the same approach to every Branford project: a thorough initial assessment, a clear written proposal with specific line items, and daily communication throughout construction.

We hold CT Home Improvement Contractor License #0668405 and carry full liability and workers' compensation insurance.

data.title: Recent New Haven County Projects
data.subtitle: A sample of recent work completed from our Orange office for homeowners near Branford.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Milford
items.description:
A Milford homeowner needed to replace dated 1990s oak cabinets and laminate countertops. We removed the peninsula, installed soft-close cabinetry with quartz countertops and subway tile, and extended LVP flooring into the dining room.

"They showed up when they said they would, cleaned up every day, and the kitchen turned out better than I imagined." — Ivana P., Milford

items.title: Whole-Home Remodeling in Hamden
items.description:
A Hamden family purchased a 1960s colonial and needed a complete renovation — kitchen, two bathrooms, new flooring throughout, and a finished basement. We managed the entire project from a single contract.

"Having one contractor handle everything made all the difference. The house feels completely new." — The Nguyen Family, Hamden

items.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Orange
items.description:
An Orange homeowner wanted to update a dated 1970s bathroom with pink tile and a fiberglass tub surround. We installed a frameless glass walk-in shower with porcelain tile, a floating vanity with quartz top, and new plumbing fixtures.

"We finally have a bathroom that feels modern. The tile work is flawless." — Mark & Lisa R., Orange

data.title: Remodeling in Branford, Connecticut
data.content:
Branford's shoreline and inland neighborhoods feature colonial, cape, and ranch-style homes built primarily between the 1950s and 1980s. Coastal properties may present additional considerations including salt air exposure and moisture management. Inland neighborhoods share the same mid-century construction patterns common throughout New Haven County.

Branford sits along the Long Island Sound shoreline east of New Haven. The town's mix of shoreline and inland properties means renovation projects can range from standard interior updates to more complex work that accounts for coastal conditions. The Branford Building Department handles permits efficiently, and we maintain a good working relationship with their team.

Branford is about 20 minutes from our Orange office. That proximity means faster response times, no travel delays, and direct familiarity with Branford's housing stock and building department requirements. When you call us about a project in Branford, you're reaching a team that already knows the conditions your home is likely to present.

data.title: Branford Building Department & Permits
data.content:
Kitchen and bathroom remodeling in Branford requires permits from the Branford Building Department for any structural, electrical, or plumbing work. Most renovation projects involve all three.

We handle all permit applications, schedule all required inspections, and coordinate the sequencing so the permit process doesn't delay your construction timeline. You never need to visit town hall or coordinate with inspectors yourself — that's included in every project scope.

Being based in Orange means we're about 20 minutes from our Orange office and can respond quickly to any inspection questions or follow-ups the Branford Building Department requires.

data.title: Our Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every project follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We look at the layout, note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions, and get a clear picture of what you want to accomplish.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. Line items are specific. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.headline: Our Orange Office

data.title: Serving Two Counties from Orange
data.subtitle: From our Orange headquarters, we serve homeowners throughout Fairfield and New Haven Counties with dedicated teams in both regions.

items.question: Do you serve Branford, CT?
items.answer: Yes. We serve Branford and all of New Haven County from our office in Orange, CT — about 20 minutes from our Orange office. Call (203) 466-9148 to schedule a free consultation.

items.question: Do I need a permit for remodeling in Branford?
items.answer: Most remodeling projects in Branford require permits from the Branford Building Department for structural, electrical, and plumbing work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project scope.

items.question: How do I schedule a consultation?
items.answer: Call (203) 466-9148 or fill out the contact form on this page. We offer both in-person and remote consultations via Google Meet. In-person visits for Branford projects are easy to schedule since we're based about 20 minutes from our Orange office. No charge, no obligation.

items.question: Are you licensed and insured?
items.answer: Yes. BuiltWell CT holds Connecticut Home Improvement Contractor license #0668405. We carry full general liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage. Copies available upon request.

items.question: How long does a typical remodeling project take?
items.answer: Timelines vary by scope. Bathroom remodels typically take 3–6 weeks, kitchen remodels 6–12 weeks, and basement finishing 4–8 weeks. We provide a detailed schedule in every written proposal.

items.question: What types of homes do you work on in Branford?
items.answer: Branford's housing stock is primarily colonial, cape, and ranch-style homes built between the 1950s through 1980s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Do you handle permits and inspections?
items.answer: Yes. Permit coordination is included in every project scope. We handle all building, electrical, and plumbing permit applications and schedule every required inspection with the Branford Building Department.

items.question: What does your proposal include?
items.answer: Every BuiltWell proposal is a detailed, line-item document that breaks out costs for each component: cabinetry, countertops, tile, plumbing, electrical, flooring, permits, and labor. No hidden fees.

items.question: Do you offer financing?
items.answer: Yes. We partner with GreenSky to offer flexible financing options. Plans include deferred interest and fixed monthly payments. We walk you through available plans during your consultation.

items.question: What happens if something unexpected comes up during construction?
items.answer: If we discover something behind a wall or under a floor — outdated wiring, water damage, code violations — we contact you that same day, explain what we found, present options with costs, and don't proceed until you approve in writing.
